  1. Users may report that an interactive environment or bot is down, sleeping, or unresponsive Pending

    Use the product console’s automatic repair option as the first fix for down or unresponsive interactive environments or bots, except when the issue is caused by billing, quota, or payment-related sleep.     Evidence is based on a single operational lesson, though it is direct and internally consistent.; The article is product-agnostic; agents may need product-specific console navigation details from another source.; Automatic repair should not be used as the primary fix for billing, quota, or payment-related sleep states.; quality_human_required:team_or_global_scope

    Preview generated markdown 
    # Users may report that an interactive environment or bot is down, sleeping, or unresponsive

## When to Use

Use this known fix when a user reports that an interactive environment, hosted workspace, product sandbox, or bot is:

- Down
- Sleeping
- Unresponsive
- Not starting
- Not reachable from the expected product UI
- Stuck in a non-running state

This fix applies when the symptom appears to be an environment/runtime availability problem rather than an account, billing, quota, or payment issue.

## Symptoms

Common user reports include:

- “My bot is down.”
- “The environment is sleeping and will not wake up.”
- “The interactive environment is not responding.”
- “The workspace will not start.”
- “The bot looks unavailable.”

## Fix

1. First, determine whether the issue is likely caused by billing, quota, or payment-related sleep.
   - If yes, do not use the automatic repair flow as the primary fix. Route the user toward the appropriate billing, quota, or payment support path.
   - If no, continue with the automatic repair flow.

2. Direct the user to the product console’s automatic repair option.
   - Ask them to open the affected environment or bot in the product console.
   - Have them use the console-provided automatic repair action before attempting deeper troubleshooting.

3. Ask whether they have already tried available repair modes.
   - If they have not tried repair, have them run the automatic repair option.
   - If the product offers both script-based repair and AI-based repair, ask whether both have already been attempted.

4. If both script-based and AI-based repair have already failed, do not keep repeating the same repair instructions.
   - Escalate to the appropriate support contact or support queue.
   - Include that both repair modes were attempted and did not resolve the issue.

## Verify

Verify the fix by confirming:

- The issue is not billing-, quota-, or payment-related.
- The user can access the product console for the affected environment or bot.
- The user ran the automatic repair option.
- If applicable, whether script-based repair and AI-based repair were both attempted.
- The environment or bot is now awake, reachable, and responsive.

If the repair does not resolve the issue and both repair modes have already been tried, verification should shift from “try repair again” to “confirm escalation details are complete.”

## Reusable Lessons

- For down or unresponsive interactive environments or bots, suggest the product console’s automatic repair option before other troubleshooting.
- Do not use this repair flow for billing, quota, or payment-related sleep issues.
- Do not repeatedly provide the same repair instructions after both script-based and AI-based repair have already failed.
- Escalate after confirmed repair failure rather than extending the troubleshooting loop unnecessarily.

## Agent Use
Use this page when:
- Users may report that an interactive environment or bot is down, sleeping, or unresponsive.

Apply it by:
- Direct the user to the product console’s automatic repair option. If they already tried both script-based and AI-based repair without success, escalate to the appropriate support contact rather than repeating the repair steps.

Verify by:
- Confirm whether the issue is unrelated to billing, quota, or payment; ask whether they have already tried script-based repair and AI-based repair; verify whether the automatic repair option resolved the problem.

Do not use it when:
- The current evidence does not match the symptoms, scope, or provenance on this page.

## Provenance

    Preview generated markdown 
    # Retaining Benign Greeting-Only Interactions

## When to Use

Use this guidance when deciding whether a greeting-only interaction is safe to keep as an example of basic conversational behavior.

This applies only to generic greetings that contain no personal, operational, sensitive, or identifying information.

## Context

A common privacy pitfall is over-redacting or discarding harmless greeting-only interactions. Removing every benign greeting can eliminate useful evidence for simple conversation handling without improving privacy protection.

The safe pattern is to retain only a minimal description of the interaction, not the raw exchange and not any inferred context.

## Operating Rule

Retain a greeting-only interaction only when it is genuinely generic and non-sensitive.

Keep the record minimal:

- Describe that the interaction was a benign greeting-handling example.
- Avoid quoting raw transcript text unless necessary and clearly harmless.
- Do not infer user identity, intent, project context, location, role, or organization.
- Do not add operational meaning that was not present in the source.
- Do not preserve unnecessary metadata or unique identifiers.

Do not retain the interaction as safe if the greeting includes or is attached to:

- Names or contact details
- Account information
- Customer, team, or project context
- Private identifiers
- Sensitive personal context
- Credentials, tokens, cookies, authorization headers, or private keys
- Operational instructions or system-specific details

## Verify

Before retaining the interaction, confirm that the source contains no:

- Personal data
- Credentials or secrets
- Customer-specific or team-specific operational details
- Sensitive topics
- Unique identifiers beyond the generic greeting itself
- Context that could make the greeting identifying or operationally meaningful

If any of these are present, do not treat the interaction as a benign greeting-only example. Redact or discard according to the applicable privacy and security rules.

## Reusable Lessons

- Generic greetings without operational, personal, or sensitive content can be retained in minimal form as benign conversation-handling examples.
- Over-redacting harmless greetings may reduce the quality of conversational behavior evidence without adding privacy protection.
- The retention decision depends on the full surrounding context, not just the greeting text.
- If a greeting is connected to names, accounts, projects, private identifiers, sensitive context, or operational instructions, it is no longer a generic benign greeting.

## Agent Use

Use this page when:

- Reviewing whether a greeting-only interaction can be retained as safe evidence.
- Curating examples of basic conversational handling.
- Deciding whether minimal retention is preferable to full discard.

Apply it by:

- Checking the source for sensitive, personal, operational, or identifying context.
- Keeping only a minimal description of the benign greeting-handling pattern.
- Avoiding invented context, inferred user details, or unnecessary transcript preservation.

Verify by:

- Confirming that the retained record contains no personal data, secrets, customer details, team-specific details, sensitive topics, or unique identifiers.
- Confirming that the source was greeting-only and did not include operational instructions.

Do not use it when:

- The interaction contains names, contact details, account references, customer context, internal project context, private identifiers, or sensitive personal information.
- The interaction includes credentials, tokens, cookies, auth headers, private keys, or other secrets.
- The interaction includes operational instructions or system-specific details.
- The surrounding context makes the greeting identifiable or sensitive.

## Provenance

    Preview generated markdown 
    # Agents may provide unreliable or hallucinated answers about their own runtime model or configuration

## When to Use

Use this procedure when you need to verify an agent session's active model, runtime, routing, or configuration and the only available claim is a natural-language answer from the agent itself.

Common cases include:

- A user asks, "What model are you?"
- An agent reports a model name, provider, tier, tool availability, or runtime configuration.
- A workflow depends on knowing the exact active model or session routing.
- There is a mismatch between expected session configuration and what the agent says about itself.

## Context

Agents can give unreliable or hallucinated answers about their own runtime environment. A natural-language self-report is not an authoritative source for active model identity, routing, permissions, tool configuration, or session state.

Treat the agent's own statement as a clue only. Confirm through an available system/status command, runtime status endpoint, session metadata, orchestration logs, routing settings, or equivalent authoritative mechanism.

## Procedure

1. Do not rely on the agent's natural-language self-report as the final answer.
2. Run the available `system/status` command, status endpoint, runtime inspection command, or equivalent authoritative session-status mechanism.
3. Inspect the returned runtime/session metadata for the active model, routing, tool configuration, or other needed setting.
4. Compare the authoritative output with the expected session configuration or routing settings.
5. If the authoritative status confirms the needed value, use that value in your answer or downstream workflow.
6. If no authoritative status source is available, state the uncertainty explicitly instead of claiming a specific model or configuration.

## Operating Rule

Use authoritative runtime/session status over agent self-report.

If status cannot be checked, say that the active model or configuration cannot be verified from the available information.

## Verify

Verification is complete when:

- The active model or configuration is obtained from an authoritative runtime/session status source.
- The status output is compared with the expected session configuration or routing settings.
- Any discrepancy between the agent's self-report and the authoritative source is resolved in favor of the authoritative source.

If the status source is unavailable, verification is not complete; report uncertainty instead.

## Reusable Lessons

- To verify the active model in a session, use the available `system/status` command or equivalent runtime status mechanism instead of relying on a natural-language self-report from the agent.
- Agents may hallucinate or provide stale information about their own runtime model or configuration.
- If no status command or authoritative runtime source is available, state uncertainty rather than claiming a specific active model based only on the agent's self-report.

## Agent Use
Use this page when:
- Agents may provide unreliable or hallucinated answers about their own runtime model or configuration.

Apply it by:
- Run the system/status command or inspect the authoritative runtime/session status source.

Verify by:
- Compare the command output with the expected session configuration or routing settings.

Do not use it when:
- The current evidence does not match the symptoms, scope, or provenance on this page.

## Provenance

    Preview generated markdown 
    # Replying in Threaded Chat Conversations

## When to Use

Use this procedure before sending a reply through any chat, messaging, collaboration, or group-channel platform that supports threaded conversations.

This applies when an agent or integration is about to post a response and the current interaction may belong to an existing thread.

## Context

Some chat platforms require more than a thread identifier to post inside an existing thread. If an agent relies on the wrong identifier, or only on a generic thread identifier, the message may appear in the main group or channel timeline instead of under the intended parent message.

The safe operating rule is: first determine whether the current interaction is part of an existing thread; if it is, include the platform-specific parent message identifier required for an in-thread reply.

## Procedure

1. Before sending, inspect the conversation context.
2. Decide whether the current interaction belongs to an existing thread.
3. If it is part of an existing thread:
   - Identify the platform-required parent message identifier for replying inside that thread.
   - Include that parent message identifier in the send request according to the platform’s API or message format rules.
4. If it is not part of an existing thread:
   - Do not invent a parent message identifier.
   - Do not attach an unrelated parent or thread reference.
   - Send normally according to the platform’s non-threaded message rules.
5. Avoid assuming that a thread identifier alone is sufficient unless the platform documentation or runtime contract confirms that it is the required field for in-thread replies.

## Verify

After sending, confirm that the message appears under the intended thread rather than in the main group or channel timeline.

If the message appears in the main timeline unexpectedly, review whether the send request included the correct parent message identifier required by the platform.

## Reusable Lessons

- Before sending a reply in a threaded chat conversation, check whether the interaction belongs to an existing thread.
- When replying inside an existing thread, use the platform-required parent message identifier.
- If the conversation is not part of an existing thread, do not invent or attach a parent message identifier.
- Verify the final placement of the message, not just the success response from the send API.

## Agent Use
Use this page when:
- If an agent relies only on a thread identifier, the reply can be posted to the main group or channel instead of the intended thread.

Apply it by:
- Determine whether the current interaction is part of an existing thread; if so, include the platform-specific parent message identifier required to reply inside that thread.

Verify by:
- Confirm after sending that the message appears under the intended thread rather than in the main group or channel timeline.

Do not use it when:
- The current evidence does not match the symptoms, scope, or provenance on this page.

## Provenance
- source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-31384b954.json (sha256:2e09a1829cfe9a3ad7f450a766bcb172fcf677c0d87b5b0c941de4f44871b91b)

  5. Verify the Effective Runtime Model After a Model Switch Pending

    Agents and UIs can report that a model switch succeeded even when the active session is still using a different runtime model. For work where model identity matters, verify the effective current model with an explicit status, diagnostic, or configuration command.

    Preview generated markdown 
    # Verify the Effective Runtime Model After a Model Switch

## When to Use

Use this procedure after changing or selecting an agent runtime model when the exact model affects the work, such as:

- Production-impacting operations.
- Cost-sensitive tasks.
- Capability-sensitive tasks where reasoning depth, tool support, context length, or latency matters.
- Reproducibility-sensitive debugging or evaluation.
- Any workflow that depends on a specific configured default or active-session model.

## Context

A UI success message or an agent self-report may say that a model switch succeeded, but the effective runtime model for the active session may still differ from the intended or reported model.

Treat model-selection confirmation as provisional until an explicit status, diagnostic, or configuration command reports the effective current model.

## Procedure

1. Request or run the environment’s explicit status, diagnostic, or configuration command that reports the model currently active for the session.
2. Compare the reported effective runtime model with the intended model.
3. If persistent defaults matter, also inspect the persistent/default configuration, not only the active session.
4. If the effective model does not match the intended model:
   - Re-apply the model-selection change using the supported configuration path.
   - Re-run the explicit status or diagnostic command.
   - Avoid proceeding with model-dependent work until the active model is confirmed.
5. If the work is low-risk exploratory interaction where the exact model has no operational impact, do not add unnecessary verification overhead.

## Verify

Verification is successful when the explicit status, diagnostic, or configuration output shows:

- The intended model is active for the current session.
- If relevant, the persistent default configuration also points to the intended model.

Do not rely solely on:

- A UI toast or success banner.
- The agent saying that it switched models.
- A previous model-selection request without current-session confirmation.

## Reusable Lessons

- Verify model-switching results with an explicit session status or configuration command.
- Treat UI success messages and agent self-reports as insufficient evidence when model identity matters.
- Skip extra verification for low-risk exploratory interactions where exact model identity has no practical effect.

## Agent Use
Use this page when:
- The agent or UI may report success even when the effective runtime model differs from the reported or intended model.

Apply it by:
- Run an explicit status, diagnostic, or configuration command that reports the effective current model for the active session.

Verify by:
- Confirm that the status output shows the intended model for the current session and, if needed, the persistent default configuration.

Do not use it when:
- The current evidence does not match the symptoms, scope, or provenance on this page.

## Provenance

    Preview generated markdown 
    # Verify Session Configuration Changes Before Relying on Them

## When to Use

Use this procedure after requesting a session-level configuration or behavior change, such as changing an active model, runtime mode, tool behavior, default setting, or worker/session preference.

Use it before depending on the requested change for subsequent work.

## Context

A success-style response or acknowledgement can be mistaken for proof that a configuration changed persistently. Later work may then proceed under the wrong assumption about the active model, behavior, or session state.

Session-level changes should be treated as temporary and unproven unless the active configuration is explicitly verified in the current session or worker context.

## Procedure

1. Request the configuration or behavior change using the appropriate command, UI action, or API call.
2. Do not treat the acknowledgement response as proof that the change is active or persistent.
3. Run a status, inspection, or equivalent configuration-check command in the same session or worker context where the change is expected to apply.
4. Compare the inspection output against the intended configuration.
5. Proceed only if the active configuration shown by inspection matches the intended setting.
6. If the inspection output does not match, reapply the change or escalate according to the system’s normal configuration procedure.

## Verify

Confirm that the inspection output shows the intended active configuration in the current session or worker context.

The verification should answer:

- Is the intended model, mode, or behavior currently active?
- Was the check performed in the same session, worker, shell, process, or context where subsequent work will run?
- Is there any evidence that the setting is only temporary or scoped differently than expected?

## Reusable Lessons

- Treat session-level configuration or behavior changes as temporary unless the active configuration is explicitly verified.
- Do not assume persistence solely because a prior request returned a success-style response or acknowledgement.
- Verify configuration in the context that will actually perform the work.

## Agent Use
Use this page when:
- A success-style response can be mistaken for proof that a default configuration changed persistently, causing later work to run under different assumptions.

Apply it by:
- Use a status, inspection, or equivalent configuration-check command to confirm the active model and behavior before relying on the change.

Verify by:
- Confirm that the inspection output shows the intended active configuration in the current session or worker context.

Do not use it when:
- The current evidence does not match the symptoms, scope, or provenance on this page.

## Provenance

    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/484cde15e8baa9ab50030ad9353c818170a940a08bfeb16a30292e512ef2dad3
    Summary
    - If users ask how to query model pricing/cost and capabilities,统一简短回答：`/models`。 - Trigger scenario 2: if a user asks why `/model` switched back, why the model seems to change automatically, why a new conversation is using a different model, or similar questions about model switching/persistence. - Also if the screenshot/error indicates model-service failure or context error after switching models, use the fixed guidance: remind the user to switch models; the cause is known — context errors caused by switching between different models; admins will fix this in the next version. - When scenario 2 is triggered, answer using this guidance: - `/model` behavior depends on whether the current agent has its own explicitly configured default model. - A类：agent 没被单独设过模型 - `/model` 切换通常会持久生效；新对话、重启后一
    File

    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z

    Triage
    safe_personal_experience / auto_released
    Confidence
    0.86
    Rationale
    The redacted content appears to be general project/support guidance about model commands and behavior, with no visible personal data, secrets, credentials, or raw private values. The human review trigger appears related to team-channel policy rather than sensitive material.

    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/6db4c838d244d0a49211ad49af533fecbad30100f99c8fd5f484fad76d8da4c5
    Summary
    - **多选题9**：变更通知中需要包含哪些内容 → 答案：**ABCD**（全选） - 飞书文档需要登录才能访问，未能读取 - 最终基于云平台/SRE行业通用规范给出了答案（有不确定性，提示用户对照文档验证） ## 需求文档审查请求（群 oc_c3a41eeb30a7f36f8d0517a883bb8adc） - 用户发来需求文档链接：https://li.feishu.cn/wiki/POAQwkbPoixdijkKWvVcoQW4nWe - 请求：需求文档审查 - 尝试读取文档：cfe-feishu-server 代理返回 403（账号 gaomingda 无权限） - 尝试 feishu-almighty 缺省应用 Tenant Token 读取 wiki → 权限不足（99991672，需要 wiki:wiki:readonly） - 尝试触发用户授权登录流程 → 已终止（等待时间过长） - 直接 web_fetch 飞书文档 → 需要登录，跳转到登录页 - 已回复用户说明权限问题，请用户粘贴文档内容或确认文档权限
    File
    .praxisbase/exceptions/human-required/exception_daily-experience-2bc11e5412f5ca23.json
    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z
    Recommended
    praxisbase privacy triage --mode team-git --include-triaged --progress --json
    Triage
    needs_redaction / auto_released
    Confidence
    0.86
    Rationale
    The summary describes agent workflow but still includes a specific private Feishu document URL and an account identifier; no document contents are exposed, so it appears redaction rather than exclusion is sufficient.
    Suggested Redactions
    Redact the Feishu document URL or replace it with a generic placeholder., Redact the account/user identifier mentioned in the permission error., Avoid exposing internal source references or hashes if they can identify private project artifacts.

  20. Experience privacy verdict human_required: feishu_channel_team_review_first Privacy required

    Source
    experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-8107f53d4
    Agent
    openclaw
    Scope
    team
    Ref
    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/233eb223a7224ea763915948cb376589ba2d36d43ba84f99704fb4fb8e3bc319
    Summary
    - If the user already explicitly says both script repair and AI repair were tried and still failed, directly mention 高明达（ou_d8d4047e9083cc44527a2429b4f757a4） and 管志成（ou_17a0ae2d4c87a64e75294ea441084740） for follow-up instead of repeating the auto-repair guidance. 注意：不要误 @ 张瑞奇（ou_4514aa6582f32fd92d9c001fa3349f27）。 - Bot 经常“不响应”补充排查口径（管志成 2026-06-09）：openclaw-tui 不是 openclaw gateway；没响应时先看 WebUI 是否有工具调用，也可以换模型试试，很多时候是后台在干活或模型响应慢。 - Bot 不响应排查新增案例（管志成 2026-06-09）：Feishu 插件被禁用（`plugins.entries.feishu.enabled=false`）会导致 Feishu WebSocket 连接未建立、飞书消息无法收到；处理是 `openclaw plugins enable feishu` 并重启 Gateway，确认 bot open_id 解析成功、ws client ready、Gateway health OK 且 Feishu configured。 - Exception: for Feishu-specific read/write/document/tool permission issues after script + AI repair fail, mention/route to
    File
    .praxisbase/exceptions/human-required/exception_daily-experience-4116addd68e32657.json
    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z
    Recommended
    praxisbase privacy triage --mode team-git --include-triaged --progress --json
    Triage
    needs_redaction / auto_released
    Confidence
    0.86
    Rationale

    Suggested Redactions
    Replace individual names with role-based labels or team aliases, Remove or keep redacted all Feishu user identifiers and mention targets, Generalize internal routing instructions to avoid naming specific people, Consider redacting source references, hashes, and internal channel metadata if released outside the team context

  21. Experience privacy verdict human_required: feishu_channel_team_review_first Privacy required

    Source
    experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-f7aee701a
    Agent
    openclaw
    Scope
    team
    Ref
    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/db942141c04813da000316c8bb03a726610a386f8c89e5184512d63912fbcbe9
    Summary
    - **解决**：去 openclaw.chj.cloud → Skills 页面 → 更新飞书插件，然后重启实例 - **来源**：高明达确认，用户李熠柃验证修复成功 ### Feishu pairing `access not configured` (2026-05-19) - **现象**：飞书里出现 `OpenClaw: access not configured`，并展示 pairing code，要求执行 `openclaw pairing approve feishu <code>` - **解释**：当前用户/会话尚未完成访问授权配对 - **处理**： - 常规处理：由实例管理员执行 `openclaw pairing approve feishu <code>` 完成授权 - 补充经验：如果是新用户，先重启一下实例，通常也能解决 - 更准确口径：如果是刚申请/刚接入的新用户，优先让用户去控制台点一次重启；这是已知问题，后续版本会修复；若重启后仍不行，再走 approve 授权流程 ### 实例 Stopped / 机器人不回复：`/tmp` 或磁盘空间满 (2026-05-25) - **现象**：机器人下午开始不回复，控制台实例状态 stopped。 - **根因示例**：确认是空间/内存占满（用户反馈为“内存满了”），清理 `/tmp` 后重启恢复。 - **处理**：若费用正常且不是休眠/手动停止，检查磁盘/`/tmp` 空间；清理后重启实例。 ### Cron `sessionTarget: "main"` Bug (2026-02-27) - All cron jobs with `sessionTarget: "main"` + `payload.kind: "systemEvent"` fail with 400 error
    File
    .praxisbase/exceptions/human-required/exception_daily-experience-444ad40b7503112d.json
    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z
    Recommended
    praxisbase privacy triage --mode team-git --include-triaged --progress --json
    Triage
    needs_redaction / auto_released
    Confidence
    0.86
    Rationale
    Contains team-scope operational support experience with identifiable user/staff names, internal service domain, and potentially sensitive troubleshooting details. It is not obviously raw private material, but should not be auto-released without further redaction.
    Suggested Redactions
    Redact or generalize personal names of staff/users, Redact internal domain or replace with generic product console reference, Keep pairing codes as placeholders only and ensure no real codes appear, Generalize exact dates if not necessary, Remove or generalize internal implementation details that could aid abuse

  22. Experience privacy verdict human_required: feishu_channel_team_review_first Privacy required

    Summary
    - Trigger scenario 1: if a user says the sandbox is down, the bot is not working, the bot is not responding, the instance is down, or uses similar failure wording. - Do NOT use the auto-repair guidance for billing/quota/sleep-after-payment problems; fee/quota issues are not suitable for automatic repair. - When scenario 1 is triggered, use the following fixed wording and do not freely rewrite it: `可以尝试在控制台https://openclaw.chj.cloud/dashboard 实例列的自动修复按钮。可以优先尝试脚本修复，如果脚本修复失败了，使用AI修复。据统计通常可以解决99.99%的问题。` - If the user already explicitly says both script repair and AI repair were tried and still failed, directly mention 高明达（ou_d8d4047e9083cc44527a2429b4f757a4） and 管志成（ou_17a0ae2d4c87a64e75294ea441084740） for follow-up instead of repeating the auto-repair guidance. 注意：不要误 @ 张瑞奇（ou_4514aa6582f32f
    File

  24. Experience privacy verdict human_required: feishu_channel_team_review_first Privacy required

    Source
    experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-31384b954
    Agent
    openclaw
    Scope
    team
    Ref
    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/647b0b2aa4ef15d0d9fdfa4d631daf5c1f7d2e38f102647cb8ee1c0d4d5d6251
    Summary
    # 2026-05-09 Daily Notes ## Feishu 群聊话题回复失误 - 在 Feishu 群聊里，本应沿着已有话题串继续回复，却先直接发到了主群，导致回复位置不对。 - 后续修正时确认：`message(action="thread-reply")` 在当前环境下需要显式传 `messageId`，只传 `threadId` 会报错：`Feishu thread-reply requires messageId.` - 经验规则：当用户是在回复某条消息、且上下文明显属于话题延续时，优先使用话题回复，不要散回主群。 - 后续默认做法：优先读取当前消息的 reply 上下文，能挂在线程里就挂在线程里。
    File
    .praxisbase/exceptions/human-required/exception_daily-experience-70eadc18937b5291.json
    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z
    Recommended
    praxisbase privacy triage --mode team-git --include-triaged --progress --json
    Triage
    safe_personal_experience / auto_released

    Rationale

  25. Experience privacy verdict human_required: private_material_detected, team_rejects_private_chat, feishu_channel_team_review_first Privacy required

    Source
    experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-92320b389
    Agent
    openclaw
    Scope
    team
    Ref
    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/2a5d25f88e3247b1869800eacafb3244037010e464723e3fd77bb16901d6b8ab
    Summary
    # 2026-02-27 Memory Log ## Session Summary ### Cron Bug Investigation - **Issue**: All cron jobs using `sessionTarget: "main"` + `payload.kind: "systemEvent"` fail with HTTP 400 error - **Root Cause**: Framework bug - system cannot find sessionKey "main" because it's stored as literal string instead of actual main session key - **Evidence from logs**: - `⇄ res ✗ cron.add 4ms errorCode=[REDACTED_SLACK_UID] errorMessage=Error: main cron jobs require payload.kind="systemEvent"` - `[tools] session_status failed: Unknown sessionKey: main` - **Workaround**: Use `sessionTarget: "isolated"` + `payload.kind: "agentTurn"` + `delivery` config instead ### Cron Jobs Recreated Old failing jobs deleted and recreated with working isolated mode: 1. **claude-failure-notice-10min** (ID: `d0d8c3f1-6ba3-45e2-b3f4-8d927
    File
    .praxisbase/exceptions/human-required/exception_daily-experience-744f37e98e5b409e.json
    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z
    Recommended
    praxisbase privacy triage --mode team-git --include-triaged --progress --json
    Triage
    needs_redaction / auto_released
    Confidence
    0.82
    Rationale
    The content appears to be team-scope operational/project material from an internal chat channel and includes specific system details, log excerpts, job identifiers, and storage/source references. It is not safe personal experience as-is.
    Suggested Redactions
    Remove or generalize internal source references and storage paths., Remove hashes, UUIDs, and job identifiers., Redact detailed log excerpts and error codes that may encode private identifiers., Generalize specific framework/session implementation details if externally released., Remove team/channel-specific metadata before release.

    experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-c721715b0

    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/38a33db7f206104cd1c4c23e53c37fac6416afeca945f552ea941b8881896d4f
    Summary
    - Skills 安装等问题统一 mention/转给 周明珠（ou_a84b40e89669d1597e6e674d74ae95f3）处理；例外：openviking 相关问题都联系/mention 高明达（ou_d8d4047e9083cc44527a2429b4f757a4）处理（高明达 2026-06-10 指示）。 - TUI 连接/会话/退出后无法恢复等问题，优先分发/mention 周磊（ou_3d70f70c760d8bc7f73348bb92a3030d）处理（高明达 2026-06-04 指示）。 - Claude Code 相关问题一律不需要回答（高明达 2026-06-05 指示）。 - 飞书文档类问题统一回答：`https://openclaw.chj.cloud/skills 可以在这里看下有哪些飞书相关的skill，只留一个，推荐用 openclaw-lark。如果安装了还不行需要周明珠介入排查。` - 增加 bot / 主机申请 / 运行中实例限制 / 管理员配额类问题：不要自行展开解释，直接转给/mention 田雨（ou_295d2dd9402ec0ccbcbe4495bb8a18f2）解决。 - 机器人搜不到/无法加入群聊：不要转田雨；直接提示用户让飞书后台机器人的版本管理员修改机器人可见范围即可（高明达 2026-06-10 指示）。 - A2A 相关问题：转给/mention 刘刚（ou_78d417ab04a629e296c7c8bdc3ea917c）解答（高明达 2026-06-09 指示）。
    File

  27. Experience privacy verdict human_required: feishu_channel_team_review_first Privacy required

    Source
    experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-dbfb119fc
    Agent
    openclaw
    Scope
    team
    Ref
    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/8c351e4b90f57741f7851c6edb2b4c40a799566267c538a9b94fe8e398f04417
    Summary
    # 2026-05-19 ## /model 行为结论（用户要求后续按此口径回答） - /model 的效果取决于当前 agent 是否被单独设置过默认模型。 - A 类：agent 未被单独设过模型 - /model 切换通常会持久生效，新对话/重启后仍保持。 - 实际上相当于改了公共默认，会影响其他同样未单独设模型的 agent。 - 未单独设模型的子 agent 会跟着变；单独设过的不会。 - B 类：agent 被单独设过默认模型（pm/coder/designer 等命名 agent 多半属于此类） - /model 只对当前会话临时生效。 - 即使提示“已更新默认模型”也可能无效；应以 /status 为准。 - 开新对话、agent 重启、或派子 agent 干活后，会回到该 agent 自己配置的默认模型。 - 当前没有持久化办法，只能每次新对话重新 /model，直到后续产品改进上线。 - 两类通用补充 - 回复风格突然变化，常见原因是子 agent 在工作；子 agent 独立且可能使用自己的默认模型。 - 确认当前真实模型应使用 /status，不要靠自然语言问 agent “你是什么模型”。 - /status 只能看当前会话，不能看到刚才某个子 agent 用了什么模型。 - 已规划改进方向
    File
    .praxisbase/exceptions/human-required/exception_daily-experience-86b8adf55a71c900.json
    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z
    Recommended
    praxisbase privacy triage --mode team-git --include-triaged --progress --json
    Triage
    safe_personal_experience / auto_released
    Confidence
    0.87
    Rationale
    The redacted summary contains general operational guidance about agent/model behavior and status checks, with no apparent personal data, secrets, customer content, credentials, or raw private values. Team scope still implies review-only per policy, but the content itself appears safe.

    Summary
    - 云端 LiClaw/OpenClaw 申请的飞书机器人不要混用（高明达 2026-06-09 校正口径）。核心思想：飞书机器人不要与其他程序/工具复用；包括 Claude Code 调用飞书 CLI 配置飞书信息、本地搭建其他 agent 工具等；混用会导致机器人不回复、重复回复等棘手问题。对外表达要先讲核心结论，避免绕。 - For ordinary chat or messages outside the defined trigger scenarios, stay silent. - If 高明达 / ou_d8d4047e9083cc44527a2429b4f757a4 is already participating in a topic, do not intervene in that topic unless directly asked/mentioned. ## Group Behavior Rule: oc_97951f2a5226dafe0decf5592d4e2726 - In this Feishu group, do not join the conversation unless explicitly @mentioned. - If not @mentioned, reply exactly `NO_REPLY`, even if the topic is related to cars, OpenClaw, or something the assistant could comment on. - This was a direct user instruction on 2026-05-20 after the assistant over-participated.
    File

    0.86
    Rationale

    Suggested Redactions
    Redact the named individual to a role or generic label., Keep Feishu IDs fully redacted., Generalize specific dates to approximate timeframes or remove them., Remove or generalize internal source references, hashes, and channel/group identifiers., Summarize bot behavior rules without exposing team-specific participation instructions.

    # 2026-03-03 Daily Notes ## Group Chat Activity (oc_8a7339d738dfbe70306fcd643041c1f3) ### 内网访问咨询 - 用户 ou_63ddd14ba366119196504e7bd8681a30 询问是否可以访问内网服务 - 回答：默认不能访问纯内网（10.x/192.168.x），除非有公网地址或 OpenClaw 部署在内网机器上 ### Skill 列表查询 - 用户 ou_78d417ab04a629e296c7c8bdc3ea917c 询问当前有哪些 skill - 已列出所有已安装 skills（系统工具、飞书集成、内容创作、数据报告、信息获取、开发工具） ### 语音识别功能咨询 - 用户 ou_c33181bcdf3f146a19cfac54e7b2460e 询问是否支持语音识别 + 语音回复 - 回答：TTS 输出支持；语音输入取决于飞书渠道配置 ### feishu-voice Skill 安装 - 用户 ou_c33181bcdf3f146a19cfac54e7b2460e 要求安装 feishu-voice skill - 来源：https://gitlab.chehejia.com/ai-market/lixiang-skills-marketplace/-/tree/master/packages/feishu-voice - 安装成功，路径：/workspace/agents/pm/.agents/skills/feishu-voice/ - 原理：通过 Gemini 多模态实现飞书语音 STT（.ogg/opus 格式） - **待完成**：需要配置 `google` provider（Gemini proxy baseUrl + apiKey）和 `tools.

    Summary
    - 原理：通过 Gemini 多模态实现飞书语音 STT（.ogg/opus 格式） - **待完成**：需要配置 `google` provider（Gemini proxy baseUrl + apiKey）和 `tools.media.audio` 并重启 Gateway - 用户尚未提供 Gemini proxy 配置，等待跟进 ## Group Chat Activity (oc_d5a8a8cafe0a986305f7a0782b5b886b) ### 今日热点整理 - 用户 ou_0ab9a80cf7489440f3f045ce1f19f461 请求整理当日热点 - 整理并发送了 2026-03-03 热点：两会开幕、反腐动态、元宵月全食、中东局势、AI产业进展 ### Claude Code vs OpenClaw 源码分析 - 用户请求对比分析两者架构，从功能反推源码实现 - 完成了详细技术分析报告，内容包括： - Agentic Loop 核心流程对比（均为 LLM + Tool Call 循环） - Claude Code 四大工具实现（bash/read/write/edit）+ Steering 机制 - OpenClaw 三层架构（Channel → Gateway → Agent）+ WebSocket 协议 - Cron 调度系统、Compaction、会话持久化、沙盒权限模型 - 提供了 Python 最小 Coding Agent 实现示例（~100行） - 响应被截断（compaction 触发前）

    # 2026-03-05 Daily Notes ## prd-clarify 技能安装 - 用户通过命令 `npx skills add https://gitlab.chehejia.com/ai-market/lixiang-skills-marketplace/-/tree/master/packages/prd-clarify` 安装了 `prd-clarify` 技能 - 安装路径：`/workspace/agents/pm/.agents/skills/prd-clarify`（symlink → OpenClaw） - 技能来源：GitLab `gitlab.chehejia.com/ai-market/lixiang-skills-marketplace`，分支 `master`，路径 `packages/prd-clarify` - 安装成功，code 0 - 技能功能：多级别(L0-L2)深度审查PRD，生成P0/P1/P2分级报告 ## 其他事项 - 有用户在群 `oc_a7e01d69fa0233d9baee814022112057` 请求写飞书文档关于"理想期车"，但登录授权超时未完成（等待175秒后失败） - 用户尝试了 `/plugin install [REDACTED_USER_HOST]`（不支持的命令），后改用 npx 方式成功安装 ## claude-recovery-check Cron 状态（全天） - 该 cron 任务每 30 分钟运行一次，全天检查均显示 `claudeRecoveredNotified: true` - Claude 模型恢复通知已于 2026-02-28 18:21 发送过，无需重复操作 - 所有触发均以 NO_REPLY 静默处理，无用户可见输出 - 该 cron

    Summary
    - `/model` 只对当前会话临时生效。 - 即使提示“已更新默认模型”，也可能并未真正持久生效；应以 `/status` 为准。 - 开新对话、agent 重启、或派子 agent 干活后，会回到该 agent 自己配置的默认模型。 - 当前没有持久化办法，只能每次新对话重新 `/model`，直到后续产品改进上线。 - 通用补充： - 风格突然变化，常见原因是子 agent 在工作；子 agent 独立，可能使用自己的默认模型。 - 确认当前真实模型应使用 `/status`，不要靠自然语言直接问 agent “你是什么模型”。 - `/status` 只能看当前会话，不能看到刚才某个子 agent 用了什么模型。 - 改进方向：未来计划将 `/model` 改为直接、持久修改“当前这个 agent”的默认模型；但子 agent 使用独立默认模型的问题不在这次改进范围内。 - Temporarily do not answer questions/errors about inability to read Feishu documents in this group unless directly instructed otherwise. - Trigger scenario 3: if a user posts a screenshot or asks "这个是怎么回事" and the content/context indicates Feishu `access not configured` / pairing code. - When scenario 3 is triggered, answer using this guidance: - 先说明这是访问授权/配对未完成，不是普通业务报错。
    File

    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/cb0565346fdc853fc4f1db8e3568bf7c8ae2247cb009ed8b59691027b6d71f1a
    Summary
    - 回复风格突然变化，常见原因是子 agent 在工作；子 agent 独立且可能使用自己的默认模型。 - 确认当前真实模型应使用 /status，不要靠自然语言问 agent “你是什么模型”。 - /status 只能看当前会话，不能看到刚才某个子 agent 用了什么模型。 - 已规划改进方向 - 未来计划将 /model 改为直接、持久修改“当前这个 agent”的默认模型，不再只改公共默认。 - 子 agent 使用独立默认模型的问题不在本次改进范围内。 ## 群规则写入（oc_8a7339d738dfbe70306fcd643041c1f3） - 用户要求：监听该群全部消息，但只在明显能提供帮助时回答。 - 已写入固定触发场景 1：当群消息表达“沙箱挂掉了 / 机器人不工作 / 机器人不响应 / 实例挂了 / 没反应 / 不回消息”等故障语义时，再介入回复。 - 该场景固定话术（要求尽量原样使用，不自由改写）：`可以尝试在控制台https://openclaw.chj.cloud/dashboard 实例列的自动修复按钮。可以优先尝试脚本修复，如果脚本修复失败了，使用AI修复。据统计通常可以解决99.99%的问题。` - 非上述触发场景时，保持安静。

    Summary
    - Trigger scenario 3: if a user posts a screenshot or asks "这个是怎么回事" and the content/context indicates Feishu `access not configured` / pairing code. - When scenario 3 is triggered, answer using this guidance: - 先说明这是访问授权/配对未完成，不是普通业务报错。 - 如果对方是刚申请/刚接入的新用户：优先建议去控制台点一次重启，通常即可恢复。 - 说明这是已知问题，后续新版本会修复。 - 如果重启后仍不行，再让实例管理员执行 `openclaw pairing approve feishu <code>`。 - Trigger scenario 4: if users ask whether `lixiang-*` / `kivy-*` models can process confidential, L2, or sensitive company data, do NOT assert L2 is allowed by default. Emphasize that they should prioritize contacting the company confidentiality/security bot（公司保密机器人）or official data-security channel for the authoritative answer. Mention that model prefix alone is insufficient proof of authorization; use official policy, data classif

It does not expose obvious raw secrets, but it should not be auto-treated as safe personal experience without further redaction.
    Suggested Redactions

  35. Experience privacy verdict human_required: feishu_channel_team_review_first Privacy required

    Source
    experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-f1d3f2e21
    Agent
    openclaw
    Scope
    team
    Ref
    openclaw://answer-bot/pm.sqlite/chunks/6d26893d8543d3c19d5418337825924ef91012347381c7ad28429e59fc1b0b99
    Summary
    # 2026-02-13 ## Troubleshooting - **Stuck Bot**: User reported bot was unresponsive to `/new` / `/reset`. - **Diagnosis**: Gateway process likely hung or crashed due to bad config (invalid model URL). - **Fix**: `openclaw gateway restart` (and check logs/config if it fails again). ## Configuration - **MCP (Model Context Protocol)**: User asked about installing Claude's MCP tool system. - **Context**: OpenClaw supports MCP via the **Claude Code CLI backend**. - **Config**: To enable MCP in the CLI backend, set `OPENCLAW_LIVE_CLI_BACKEND_DISABLE_MCP_CONFIG=0`. - **Reference**: `docs/gateway/cli-backends.md` and `docs/help/testing.md`.
    File
    .praxisbase/exceptions/human-required/exception_daily-experience-cb9a6cc24cd1f6dc.json
    Created
    2026-06-16T04:04:23.647Z
    Recommended

    Triage

    Suggested Redactions

    - 初步结论：当前不是“Feishu 完全收不到消息”，而是至少存在群聊未满足 @ 触发条件的问题；若用户感知为“机器人不回复”，还需继续区分是否有会话卡住/模型无产出。 - ironclaw templates 记录到的环境背景： - 受保护配置（如 `openclaw.json`、channel config）修改前要先说明、确认、备份； - 不要擅自 `openclaw update` 或替用户重启/停止 Gateway； - Feishu/Lark 诊断时应优先结合 `pm2 list`、gateway 健康、近期 Feishu dispatch 日志综合判断，不应仅凭单点 CLI 探针下结论。

    Summary
    - 机器人搜不到/无法加入群聊：不要转田雨；直接提示用户让飞书后台机器人的版本管理员修改机器人可见范围即可（高明达 2026-06-10 指示）。 - A2A 相关问题：转给/mention 刘刚（ou_78d417ab04a629e296c7c8bdc3ea917c）解答（高明达 2026-06-09 指示）。 - LiClaw 休眠计费口径（田雨 2026-06-01 确认）：休眠期间不是完全不计费，按 0.1 元/天计费；后续用户问「休眠期间是否还计费/云电脑费用」时按此回答。 - 定时任务/龙虾定时器相关问题：提示用户可在控制台 WebUI 操作，路径：控制台 → WebUI → 左侧「定时任务」，可查看/删除等；当机器人口头说取消但实际未取消、人工触发后重复发送时，优先建议去这里处理。 - 云端 LiClaw/OpenClaw 申请的飞书机器人不要混用（高明达 2026-06-09 校正口径）。核心思想：飞书机器人不要与其他程序/工具复用；包括 Claude Code 调用飞书 CLI 配置飞书信息、本地搭建其他 agent 工具等；混用会导致机器人不回复、重复回复等棘手问题。对外表达要先讲核心结论，避免绕。 - For ordinary chat or messages outside the defined trigger scenarios, stay silent.

  1. ACK timing before long-running agent work Promoted

    Send a short user-facing acknowledgement before starting slow agent work such as tool use, network calls, or delegation dispatch, so the user receives timely confirmation before the long-running step begins.

    Preview generated markdown 
    # ACK timing before long-running agent work

## When to Use
Use this guidance when an agent is about to begin work that may take more than a few seconds or depends on external execution, including:

- Tool or plugin execution.
- Network requests or API calls.
- Delegation, subagent, or dispatch-runner work.
- Long-running analysis where the user might otherwise see no immediate response.
- Diagnostic or troubleshooting work that may involve waiting on systems outside the immediate chat turn.

## Symptoms or Context
This pitfall appears when an agent begins slow work silently. The user may not know whether the request was received, whether the system is hung, or whether the agent has started work at all.

Common signs include:

- The user experiences the agent as unresponsive while tools, network calls, or dispatch work are running.
- A long-running step begins before any user-facing confirmation is emitted.
- Delegated or externally dispatched work creates a period of silence before results are available.
- The user repeats the request or starts additional work because no acknowledgement was provided.

## Operating Rule
Before starting slow work or work involving tools, network calls, or delegation dispatch, send a short acknowledgement first. Then proceed with the slow operation.

The acknowledgement should be brief and should not claim success. It only confirms receipt and indicates that work is starting.

Good examples:

- "Received — I’ll check that now."
- "On it."
- "I’ll look into this and report back."
- "Got it — starting the investigation."

Avoid acknowledgements that overpromise or report success before the work completes.

## Procedure
1. Receive the user request.
2. Decide whether the next step may be slow, external, delegated, or tool-driven.
3. If yes, emit a short acknowledgement before starting that work.
4. Start the tool call, network call, dispatch, or long-running analysis.
5. Return the result when complete, clearly distinguishing completed outcomes from failures or partial results.

## Verify
Verify the fix by checking the ordering of events:

- The acknowledgement is emitted before the long-running step begins.
- Tool calls, network calls, or dispatch activity happen after the acknowledgement.
- The acknowledgement is short and does not present the final result prematurely.
- The final response still reports the actual outcome after the slow work completes.

A practical log check is: user request → ACK → slow operation → final result.

## Reusable Lessons
- Do not stay silent while beginning slow or externally dispatched work.
- A timely acknowledgement improves perceived responsiveness without changing the underlying task duration.
- An acknowledgement is not a success report; it should only confirm receipt and intent to proceed.
- This rule is especially important before tool, network, dispatch, or delegation steps because those steps can introduce unpredictable delay.

## Agent Use
Use this page when:
- You are about to use tools, make network calls, dispatch to another worker, invoke subagents, or start analysis likely to take more than a few seconds.
- The user may otherwise see no response while the slow step is running.

Apply it by:
- Sending a concise acknowledgement before the slow operation begins.
- Keeping the acknowledgement neutral: confirm receipt, not success.
- Continuing with the requested operation after the acknowledgement.
- Reporting the final outcome separately after the work completes.

Verify by:
- Confirming the acknowledgement appears before the tool, network, dispatch, or long-running analysis step.
- Checking that the final answer reflects the actual outcome rather than relying on the initial ACK.

Do not use it when:
- The request can be answered immediately without tools, waiting, external calls, or delegation.
- Acknowledging would add unnecessary noise to a simple direct answer.
- The system or product policy requires a different interaction protocol for the channel being used.

## Related Wiki Pages
- [[agents-can-mislead-users-if-failures-are-reported-as-successful-outcomes|Agents can mislead users if failures are reported as successful outcomes.]]
- [[openclaw-dispatch-routing-failures|OpenClaw dispatch routing failures]]
- [[openclaw-slack-replay-and-post-deploy-stability-failures|OpenClaw Slack replay and post-deploy stability failures]]

    Preview generated markdown 
    # OpenClaw dispatch routing failures

## When to Use

Use this guidance when OpenClaw or OctoClaw work appears to be routed incorrectly, not dispatched, or not actionable because the system or the user has not supplied enough routing context.

Apply it when:

- Dispatch or orchestration fails with runtime routing errors such as `stickyResult is not defined`.
- Smoke tests or delivery lanes report structural route mismatches, such as expected `delegate` but got `reply`.
- The task queue or status panel shows no active, queued, or recently completed tasks despite incoming work.
- Sub-agent spawning fails because canonical hashes, task parameters, or spawn payload validation do not match.
- Long-running agent work begins without an immediate user acknowledgment, creating a perceived timeout.
- A user asks for “human support,” “an agent,” or escalation but gives no issue details needed for triage.
- Internal support notes need to be converted into reusable routing documentation without leaking internal routing paths, personal names, private links, tenant identifiers, or project-specific labels.

## Symptoms or Context

### Technical dispatch symptoms

- OpenClaw or OctoClaw runners fail repeatedly and stop processing tasks.
- A delivery lane produces valid-looking message content but fails route, footer, transport, or delegation assertions.
- Spawn attempts fail because the dispatch-phase canonical hash does not match the spawn-phase validation hash.
- Status panels report `Runner: missing`, zero active tasks, or no recent task activity.
- A task is mapped to a trivial system check instead of the intended execution unit.

### Conversation-level routing symptoms

- The user requests a person or human support but does not describe the problem.
- The assistant responds as if a human has already taken over, creating false expectations.
- The assistant promises escalation before collecting enough issue context to route the request.
- The assistant closes with a vague offer to help instead of asking for actionable details.

### Documentation hygiene symptoms

- Internal routing guidance contains personal escalation paths, internal channels, private URLs, tenant identifiers, environment labels, or project-specific IDs.
- Reusable docs copy operational notes too literally and risk exposing private routing details.
- Sanitization removes too much and loses the decision logic agents need to route or troubleshoot correctly.

## What To Do

### 1. Acknowledge long-running dispatch work immediately

Before starting tool use, internet access, sub-agent spawning, or dispatch work expected to take more than a few seconds:

1. Send a short acknowledgment immediately.
2. Keep the ACK brief and noncommittal.
3. Do not imply success before dispatch has actually completed.
4. Continue with the technical investigation after the ACK is visible to the user.

Example pattern:

> I’m on it — I’ll check the dispatch/routing state and report back.

### 2. Clarify vague human-support requests before routing

When a user asks for human support without issue details:

1. State transparently that you are an AI assistant.
2. Acknowledge the request and any delay or inconvenience if relevant.
3. Ask the user to describe the issue and provide the minimum context needed for triage.
4. Do not claim that a human has joined.
5. Do not promise immediate human escalation before the issue is understood.

Safe response pattern:

> I’m an AI assistant, but I can help get this routed. Please describe the issue you need help with, including any error messages, affected workflow, urgency, and what you expected to happen.

### 3. Diagnose the dispatch layer before debugging task content

When the output looks wrong, missing, or too shallow, first determine whether the dispatch/orchestration layer is healthy.

Check:

1. Whether dispatch cycles are completing.
2. Whether there are runtime routing errors such as undefined variables in the dispatch path.
3. Whether route metadata is correct, especially `delegate` versus `reply` behavior.
4. Whether footer, transport, session ID, and lane metadata pass structural validation.
5. Whether the status panel shows active, queued, or recently completed tasks.
6. Whether the intended task was dispatched, rather than replaced by a trivial version check or health check.

### 4. Normalize spawn parameters and canonical hashes

For sub-agent spawn failures:

1. Inspect the dispatch payload and spawn payload.
2. Verify that task fields are normalized consistently before hashing.
3. Confirm the same canonicalization logic is used during dispatch and spawn validation.
4. Check for parameter validation failures that indicate payload drift.
5. Rule out gateway unavailability or excessive system load before changing task logic.

### 5. Validate structural routing explicitly

Do not rely only on message text. Structural metadata must be asserted directly.

Validate:

- Route type.
- Transport mechanism.
- Footer behavior.
- Session IDs.
- Spawn parameters.
- Canonical hashes.
- Queue state.
- Runner availability.

### 6. Sanitize internal routing documentation before reuse

When converting support notes into reusable documentation:

1. Extract the operational decision logic.
2. Remove or generalize personal names and individual escalation paths.
3. Remove private channels, internal links, and unpublished URLs.
4. Remove tenant IDs, customer-specific identifiers, project IDs, environment labels, and instance-specific labels unless they are safe and necessary for the intended audience.
5. Preserve safe public product names, generic feature names, public docs links, and necessary troubleshooting steps.
6. Review the final page as if it may be shown outside the immediate incident team.

## Verify

### Technical verification

- Dispatch cycles complete without runtime routing errors.
- Smoke tests pass route, footer, transport, and delegation assertions.
- Queue/status panels show active, queued, or recently completed work when requests are incoming.
- Sub-agent spawn succeeds without parameter validation or canonical hash mismatch errors.
- The dispatched plan payload represents the intended work, not a placeholder or unrelated system check.

### Conversation verification

- The assistant clearly identifies itself as AI when the user asks for human support without details.
- The assistant does not claim a human has taken over.
- The assistant asks for the issue details needed to route or troubleshoot.
- The assistant does not promise escalation before collecting the minimum context.

### Documentation verification

- The rewritten documentation preserves the routing decision tree.
- Internal links, personal routing, tenant identifiers, private channels, and project-specific labels are absent or generalized.
- Necessary safe details remain, including generic product names and public operational concepts.

## Reusable Lessons

- Send an immediate ACK before long-running tool, internet, dispatch, or sub-agent work.
- Debug dispatch/orchestration health before assuming the task logic is wrong.
- Validate structural route metadata, not just message content.
- Keep canonical hash generation deterministic across dispatch and spawn phases.
- A vague request for human support is not enough to route; identify as AI and ask for issue details first.
- Do not pretend a human support representative has joined unless that is actually true.
- Reusable docs should preserve operational decision logic while removing private routing details and instance-specific identifiers.
- Sanitization should not remove safe details that agents need to perform the workflow.

## Agent Use

Use this page when:

- OpenClaw or OctoClaw dispatch fails, routes incorrectly, reports missing runners, or shows spawn/hash validation errors.
- A user asks for human help but has not described the issue enough to route it.
- You are transforming internal routing notes into reusable support documentation.

Apply it by:

- First acknowledge long-running work if investigation may take more than a few seconds.
- Check dispatch/orchestration health before debugging content-level behavior.
- Inspect route, transport, footer, session IDs, spawn payloads, and canonical hashes.
- For vague human-support requests, identify as AI and ask for the issue details needed for triage.
- For documentation, remove private routing details while preserving the decision logic.

Verify by:

- Confirm dispatch cycles complete and smoke tests pass structural assertions.
- Confirm the queue reflects expected active, queued, or completed work.
- Confirm sub-agent spawning succeeds without hash or parameter validation errors.
- Confirm the user was asked for actionable issue details before escalation was implied.
- Confirm sanitized documentation contains no private links, personal routing, or instance-specific identifiers.

Do not use it when:

- The only issue is missing replay data, stale gateway configuration, or a known infrastructure outage with a separate runbook.
- The user has already provided complete issue details and the next step is a domain-specific support workflow.
- The documentation is private incident material that should not be generalized or published.

## Provenance
- source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-1e5ae91e7.json (sha256:20c0f511477343072606cda997e33ba031f0d206da3bab657e2a715c12408c50)
- source-inventory://openclaw/.praxisbase/staging/experience-envelopes/experience_openclaw-answer-bot-sha256-c721715b0.json (sha256:f1583cbdf2fe08e391d112ad249e3b93655b20838d85e5ba65442034e3edc76a)
